use std::sync::Mutex;

struct BufferPool {
    buffers: Mutex<Vec<Vec<u8>>>,
}

impl BufferPool {
    fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            buffers: Mutex::new(Vec::new()),
        }
    }

    fn acquire(&self) -> Vec<u8> {
        let mut pool = self.buffers.lock().unwrap();
        pool.pop().unwrap_or_else(|| Vec::with_capacity(8192))
    }

    // BAD: returns buffer without shrinking oversized allocations
    fn release(&self, buf: Vec<u8>) {
        let mut pool = self.buffers.lock().unwrap();
        pool.push(buf);
    }
}

fn handle_request(pool: &BufferPool) {
    let mut buf = pool.acquire();
    // Small write that uses ~200 bytes
    buf.extend_from_slice(b"small response payload");
    process(&buf);
    // Buffer with 64KB capacity returned to pool, never shrunk
    pool.release(buf);
}
